author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! ruci nahe kena kṛṣṇa⋅nāma
tabe jāni pūrva⋅janme, āche kata pāpa⋅karme,
se lāgi’ vidhātā tore bāma

jadi anya kathā pāo, ā̐ṭiyā sā̐ṭiyā koo,
‘kṛṣṇa⋅nāma’ loite ālisa
jadi śuna ‘kṛṣṇa⋅kathā’, vajra jena paḍe māthā,
ghume jhume tallāsa’ bāliśa

jadi haya asat⋅kathā, ghumete ciyāo tathā,
śunite bāḍaye kata rati
nīca⋅saṅge sadā vāsa, sādhu-jana dekhi’ hāsa,
kulaṭā vandiyā ninda satī

śrāddha⋅deva⋅adhikārī, bhāṅgibe e bhāribhuri,
āsi’ dūta loibe bāndhiyā
ki gumāna kara deha, paci’ gali’ jā’be eha,
dhana⋅jana rahibe paḍiyā

je⋅sukhe ha’yecha matta, bujhi’ dekha tā’ra tattva,
ihā tora rahibe kothāya?
āji mara, mara kāli, maraṇa e nahe gāli,
‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha, dina jāya

je⋅kaile se⋅kaile mana, ebe hoo sacetana,
phire baisa ke tore hārāya
kahe premānanda sukhe, ‘rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa’ bala mukhe,
śamana jiniyā uṭha nāya
en_translation:
O my mind! Why do you have no taste for the Name of Lord Kṛṣṇa? I know that due to many sinful actions in your previous births, your destiny is unfavorable toward you.

If you find other topics to discuss, you speak with great effort, but you are lazy to utter the Name of Kṛṣṇa. If you hear topics about Lord Kṛṣṇa, it feels as if a thunderbolt has struck your head, and you immediately search for a pillow to sleep.

If there is unrighteous talk, you are wide awake and listen with great attachment. You always reside with the wicked and laugh at the saintly. You praise the unchaste and criticize those who are faithful.

The master of funeral rites will eventually break this illusion when his messengers come to bind you. What pride do you have in this body? It will only rot and decompose, while your wealth and family will remain behind.

The happiness you are currently intoxicated with—understand its true nature. Where will it be for you in the end? You may die today or tomorrow; death is not just an insult, it is a reality. Chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" as the days pass by.

Whatever you have done in the past, O mind, now become conscious. Return to the right path—who then can defeat you? Premānanda says: "Chant 'Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa!' with your mouth, conquer death, and rise up!"
